methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $15M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: SendCutSend
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
Foundry Lab is a Series A robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $15M+ to date. The relevant comp universe consists of nine private peers (including Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, Bedrock Robotics, and Dexterity) and one public comparable, reflecting limited but meaningful reference points in the robotics and manufacturing sector.

The stage-progression method is the primary applicable triangulation approach. Using the $15M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement yields an implied valuation of $30M. The peer-median method proved inapplicable due to insufficient data density: while seven same-sector peers exist, fewer than three peers qualified across same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier filters. Relying on a broader all-sector median would introduce undue noise.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $26M to $34M, centered on $30M median. This single-method result creates meaningful uncertainty; triangulation across multiple methodologies would strengthen conviction. Key unresolved variables include Foundry Lab's relative technology differentiation versus named peers, burn rate trajectory and runway implications, and whether the company's specific applications (manufacturing automation versus logistics, for example) command premium or discount valuations within the robotics universe. Revenue scale and unit economics, where available, would materially refine this band.
